_meta.purpose: Loop 8 hand-labeled fixture for the capture-gate v2→v3 confusion matrix and the EXIT-CRITERION false-reject measurement. Each item is a candidate correction that the v2 gate REJECTED (replayed from the live ~/.agent-recall corpus, 2026-06-21). We hand-label the GROUND TRUTH: was this a genuine soft correction the palace should have kept, or true noise it should drop?

labels.genuine_soft: A real behavioral correction / directive / corrective-fact the agent should remember. v2 wrongly rejected it (false-reject). v3 SHOULD now accept.
labels.true_noise: System fragment, pure acknowledgment, doc/report header, or pasted transcript with no behavioral rule. Both v2 and v3 MUST keep rejecting (precision floor).
labels.ambiguous: Borderline — excluded from the strict exit-criterion denominators; reported separately.

_meta.exit_criterion: On the 30 hand-labeled items (genuine_soft + true_noise, ambiguous excluded), false-reject rate must drop from ~60% toward <=15% WITHOUT re-admitting any of the 14 true_noise items.
_meta.honesty_note: Labels are this worker's judgement on the real corpus, anchored to the Loop-7 round-table examples (the button-full-width and mcp-tools cases are verbatim from the loop record). The pool is MIXED: at least one writer (the `correct` subcommand + MCP `check` paths) is NOT pre-filtered by the hook's CORRECTION_PATTERNS, so some items are agent/system pastes rather than human corrections. Agent-paraphrase caveat applies — some texts are the agent's own restatement of a correction, not the user's verbatim words.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: User tested both versions and chose the OLD static React SPA (CDN React + Babel) over the new Next.js build. The old dashboard's design, UX, and warm theme are what the team needs. Next.js is deprecated for APQC — always modify the old dashboard directly.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: sub2api (Wei-Shaw/sub2api) already has all of this built-in: API keys, billing, scheduling, payments. Don't duplicate — use sub2api as the backend, Prismma website is marketing only.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: Wrong layer — HTMLs are generated by Claude Code Routines, not locally. Novada insights must be added directly to the routine prompts via RemoteTrigger API.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: Codex has ZERO context/memory. Prompts must be extremely explicit and detailed — file paths, exact code, expected outputs. Codex can't infer what Opus knows.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: Keyless paths (template render + scaffold) are the default UX; LLM is opt-in via --llm. Tool must work cold with no key — original Plywood pattern was hand-written; tool should make hand-writing easier, not force model use.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: When AR is absent, emit a one-time recommendation pointing at the AgentRecall repo (github.com/Goldentrii/AgentRecall, npm: agent-recall-mcp) so users can discover and install it. Use marker file to avoid nagging.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: plywood-announcement.html is already bilingual with EN/中文 toggle (every paragraph has .lang-en + .lang-zh classes, setLang() flips data-lang). plywood-announcement-zh.html is redundant — replace with a redirect stub.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: Show BOTH Opus 4.7 and 4.8 — keep the full Opus lineup even when prices match

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: Identifiers are sequential two-letter codes (ar → as → at), not arbitrary labels. Always check the last entry and increment by one letter.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: No dark backgrounds for new products. Always use light mode default.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: User wanted it eventually, but the initial ask was simpler. Default to simple first, offer elaborate as option. Also: HTML content must match the source MD exactly — don't invent tasks.

items.label: genuine_soft
items.text: Product names are novada-search (not novada-mcp) and novada-proxy (not proxy4agent). KR-2 competitors are BrightData/Oxylabs/Decodo (not Firecrawl/Apify).
